examples.title: Basic Class with Public and Private Modifiers
examples.description: Demonstrates a simple class with public and private access modifiers in C++
examples.explanation: This example shows a Student class with private data members (name, age, gpa) that cannot be accessed directly from main(). Public member functions provide controlled access to the private data through getters and setters. The private modifier encapsulates the data, protecting it from unauthorized modification.

examples.title: Protected Modifier with Inheritance
examples.description: Demonstrates protected access modifier and inheritance in C++
examples.explanation: This example demonstrates the protected access modifier. Protected members are accessible within the class and by derived classes, but not from outside the class. The Student and Teacher classes inherit from Person and can access the protected members name and age directly. This allows inheritance while still restricting direct access from external code.
